parts.answer: • calculation of moles of sodium carbonate • use of 2:1 ratio to gives moles of HCl in 22.65 cm • concentration of hydrochloric acid in mol dm −3 (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation mol Na CO = 25.0 × 0.105 ÷ 1000 = 2.625 × 10 −3 / 0.002625 2 × 2.625 × 10 −3 = 5.25 × 10 −3 5.25 × 10 −3 × 1000 ÷ 22.65 = 0.23179 / 2.3179 × 10 −1 (mol dm −3 ) The reacting volumes transposed gives the final concentration of hydrochloric acid = 0.19026 (mol dm −3 ). This scores (2) The same volume used twice will give the final concentration of hydrochloric acid = 0.2100 (mol dm −3 ). These score (2) TE at each stage Ignore SF except 1 SF Allow any correct method Correct answer with some working scores (3) (3) Expert (Total for Question 4 = 9 marks) (Total for Paper = 50 marks) Pearson Education Limited.
